First, I remember that the standard way to find the Longest Increasing Subsequence (LIS) is using dynamic programming. But here, not only do I need the length, but also the count of such subsequences. So I need to track two things for each element in the array: the length of the longest subsequence ending at that element, and the number of ways to form that subsequence.

Let me think. For each index i, I can have two arrays: one for lengths (let's call it dp) and one for counts (call it counts). The idea is that dp[i] will store the length of the longest subsequence ending at i, and counts[i] will store the number of such subsequences.

For example, when processing nums[i], I look at all previous elements j < i where nums[j] < nums[i]. For each such j, if the subsequence ending at j is of length k, then adding nums[i] to that subsequence would form a subsequence of length k+1. But I need to find the maximum possible length among all these possibilities.

So, for each i, I initialize dp[i] to 1 (since the element itself is a subsequence of length 1) and counts[i] to 1 (only one way to have that single element). Then, for each j < i, if nums[j] < nums[i], I check if dp[j] + 1 is greater than the current dp[i]. If it is, then I update dp[i] and set counts[i] to counts[j]. If there are multiple j's that give the same length (i.e., same maximum length), then I need to add their counts. For example, if two different j's lead to the same maximum length for dp[i], then counts[i] should be the sum of counts[j1] + counts[j2].

Once I have filled the dp and counts arrays, I need to find the maximum length in dp. Then, for all indices where dp[i] equals that maximum length, I sum their counts[i] values. That sum is the total number of longest increasing subsequences.

public int findNumberOfLIS(int[] nums) {
    int n = nums.length;
    if (n == 0) return 0;
    int[] lengths = new int[n];
    int[] counts = new int[n];
    Arrays.fill(lengths, 1);
    Arrays.fill(counts, 1);
    
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        for (int j = 0; j < i; j++) {
            if (nums[j] < nums[i]) {
                if (lengths[j] + 1 > lengths[i]) {
                    lengths[i] = lengths[j] + 1;
                    counts[i] = counts[j];
                } else if (lengths[j] + 1 == lengths[i]) {
                    counts[i] += counts[j];
                }
            }
        }
    }
    
    int maxLen = 0;
    for (int l : lengths) {
        maxLen = Math.max(maxLen, l);
    }
    
    int res = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        if (lengths[i] == maxLen) {
            res += counts[i];
        }
    }
    return res;
}

class Solution {
    public int findNumberOfLIS(int[] nums) {
        int n = nums.length;
        if (n == 0) return 0;
        int[] lengths = new int[n];
        int[] counts = new int[n];
        Arrays.fill(lengths, 1);
        Arrays.fill(counts, 1);
        
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            for (int j = 0; j < i; j++) {
                if (nums[j] < nums[i]) {
                    if (lengths[j] + 1 > lengths[i]) {
                        lengths[i] = lengths[j] + 1;
                        counts[i] = counts[j];
                    } else if (lengths[j] + 1 == lengths[i]) {
                        counts[i] += counts[j];
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        
        int maxLen = 0;
        for (int l : lengths) {
            maxLen = Math.max(maxLen, l);
        }
        
        int res = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            if (lengths[i] == maxLen) {
                res += counts[i];
            }
        }
        return res;
    }
}

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int findNumberOfLIS(int[] nums) {
        int n = nums.length;
        if (n == 0) return 0;
        int[] lengths = new int[n];
        int[] counts = new int[n];
        Arrays.fill(lengths, 1);
        Arrays.fill(counts, 1);
        
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            for (int j = 0; j < i; j++) {
                if (nums[j] < nums[i]) {
                    if (lengths[j] + 1 > lengths[i]) {
                        lengths[i] = lengths[j] + 1;
                        counts[i] = counts[j];
                    } else if (lengths[j] + 1 == lengths[i]) {
                        counts[i] += counts[j];
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        
        int maxLen = 0;
        for (int l : lengths) {
            maxLen = Math.max(maxLen, l);
        }
        
        int res = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            if (lengths[i] == maxLen) {
                res += counts[i];
            }
        }
        return res;
    }
}
```
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: MEDIUM
explanation: Uses dynamic programming to track the length of the longest increasing subsequence ending at each index and the count of such subsequences. For each element, it checks all previous elements to update lengths and counts. Aggregates results for the maximum length subsequences. Strengths include correctness for all cases, but the O(n²) time complexity may be a weakness for very large inputs.
